title: WebNami vs Squarespace — A Free, Open-Source Blogging Alternative
description: Squarespace costs $16–$49/month and locks your content away. WebNami is free, open source, and gives you a fast static blog you fully own.

hero.heading: Beautiful blogging without the Squarespace subscription
hero.subheading: Squarespace has gorgeous templates but charges $16–$49/month, makes it difficult to leave, and runs on a slow dynamic server. WebNami is free, open source, and generates lightning-fast static pages — with your content stored as plain files you always own.

reasons.heading: Stop paying monthly
reasons.body: Squarespace's cheapest plan is $16/month — $192/year for a blog. WebNami is free and open source. Host your static site on Netlify or Vercel at no cost.

reasons.heading: Take your content with you
reasons.body: Squarespace exports a limited XML file that doesn't work cleanly on other platforms. WebNami's posts are standard Markdown — paste them anywhere, instantly.

reasons.heading: Static speed vs dynamic lag
reasons.body: Squarespace serves pages from a dynamic server. WebNami pre-builds everything into static HTML that loads in milliseconds, with no server processing on each visit.

reasons.heading: Full code control
reasons.body: Squarespace's customization is limited to their editor. WebNami is open source — modify themes, add shortcodes, tweak every detail of your blog with full access to the code.
